name: agent-index-core
description: Core infrastructure for the agent-index system. v3.9.1: raw-URL normalization (closes the remaining half of the fetch-cache bug class, finding F7) — the agent-index.json template and a new apply-updates Step 1.5 Migration 3 rewrite refs/heads/main raw.githubusercontent URLs to the /main/ short-form; the fetch layer strips query params on the refs/heads form and serves long-stale bytes (observed weeks-old directories despite cache-busters), silently breaking pin validation and binary sha verification. Idempotent, heals every install on next @ai:update. v3.9.0: member spaces move to members' own My Drive (owner-sovereign sharing; closes finding F12 — Shared-Drive folders can only be shared by drive Managers, so non-admin owners could never apply owned-content grants). member-bootstrap 3.2.0 defines the ensure-my-drive-space subroutine (Agent-Index-Private at id:root, resolved-ID rule, content migration from legacy /members/ spaces, handshake file); org-setup 3.5.0 and apply-updates 3.9.0 (Step 1.5 Migration 2) reuse it — existing members migrate automatically on next @ai:update; invite-member 1.6.0 stops creating member folders (registry member_folder_id null until reconcile); publish-updates 3.7.0 Step 6d reconciles handshakes into the registry every run; remove-member 1.2.0 adds the mandatory departure acknowledgment (owner shares SURVIVE removal — org loses governance, not recipient access; pointers annotated owner_departed; adoption path surfaced) and narrows grant revocation to the artifacts dir (+ legacy member dir if present). standards.md Addressing updated (custody/cooperation note; soft-delete nuance). Also retro-documents permission-helper-go 0.4.0 (bare id:{folderId} spec resources + browser-visible fatal errors; bug 20260604-8d20ea22-164642-e046). v3.8.1: apply-updates 3.8.1 adds Step 1.5 member-state self-heal — standing idempotent migrations that run on every @ai:update even when no entries are pending. Migration 1 caches member_folder_id from members-registry.json into member-index.json for existing members (the 3.8.0 ensure-cached logic in org-setup/member-bootstrap only reaches new members; post-release finding F3, owned-content release). v3.8.0: ID-anchored addressing & member-folder identity — standards.md addressing/soft-delete/pointer-index conventions; invite-member 1.5.0 records member_folder_id in members-registry.json at invite time; org-setup 3.4.0 + member-bootstrap 3.1.0 cache it into member-index.json; companion to gdrive adapter 2.5.0 (id:{folderId}/ anchors, stat returns id). Admin backfill required for pre-3.8.0 members (see CHANGELOG). v3.7.6: cascade-cleanup release closing the four-bug cluster discovered during 3.7.4 verification. (1) permission-helper-go 0.2.0 → 0.3.0 — binary now accepts v1.1 spec format and threads inherit:false through to Drive's InheritedPermissionsDisabled (the entire feature shipped in core 3.7.3 had never executed in production); also writes an atomic outcome JSON file alongside the spec (closes bugs 20260527-8d20ea22 + 20260527-8d20ea22-2). (2) org-setup 3.3.0 → 3.3.1 — corrects the canonical setup-responses path at two call sites (closes the spec half of bug 20260527-8d20ea22-4). (3) apply-updates 3.7.0 → 3.7.1 — Phase 4.5 manifest-sync subroutine extended with step 9 (org-mandated values reconciliation); CURRENT_SUBROUTINE_REVISION bumped 2→3 so the sweep fires on every existing install (closes the data half of bug 20260527-8d20ea22-4). (4) create-org 3.1.0 → 3.1.1 — new Step 4.5 grants all@{domain} reader on /CLAUDE.md, /org-config.json, /members-registry.json at install time (closes the spec half of bug 20260527-8d20ea22-3). (5) invite-member 1.3.0 → 1.4.0 — Category B share-set extended with the same three root-level org-readable files so new members on existing orgs get the grants in their onboarding spec. v3.7.5: companion follow-up to 3.7.4 — invite-member 1.2.0 → 1.3.0 ships Category B direct-share grants (closes the gap in bug 20260522-8d20ea22 that 3.7.4 left open). v3.7.2: cleanup patch — apply-updates 3.5.0 syncs agent-index.json adapter_version from adapter.json during Phase 3 (closes bundle-vs-config-adapter-drift idea); remove-member 1.1.0 revokes the explicit ACLs invite-member granted via permission-helper (closes bug 20260513-8d20ea22-3); view-audit 1.1.0 surfaces working folder URLs + admin audit URLs instead of dead Drive Activity URLs (closes bug 20260513-8d20ea22-2); permission-helper validate.js cleaned of trailing debris (republished via Step 0 sync). Companion: marketplace 2.5.0 adds contract-aware surfacing; developer 1.3.1 adds JS-integrity heuristic to preflight-cli that catches the debris class. v3.7.1: trigger-fix patch. (1) Phase 4.5 sentinel — adds a CURRENT_SUBROUTINE_REVISION constant + manifest_sync_subroutine_revision[] tracking field so future subroutine-step additions automatically re-fire on existing installs (closes bug 20260512-8d20ea22 — the 3.7.0 step 7 didn’t trigger on installs with pre-populated manifest_sync). (2) publish-updates writes back to org-config.installed_collections[] after each successful publish — fixes the longstanding drift where infrastructure version bumps never advanced these entries (closes bug 20260512-8d20ea22-2). apply-updates 3.4.0 -> 3.4.1; publish-updates 3.3.0 -> 3.4.0. v3.7.0: release-hygiene + admin-UX cleanup release. Five items: (1) apply-updates.md Edge Cases section restored from long-standing truncation; (2) member-index installed[].version drift fix — org-setup spec clarified, Phase 4.5 subroutine extended to reconcile data on next sync; (3) check-updates Available-to-Install section (in marketplace 2.4.0); (4) admin disambiguation prompt for ambiguous "check for updates" routing; (5) preflight CLI in developer 1.3.0 for release-script-runs-preflight integration. apply-updates 3.3.1 -> 3.4.0; org-setup 3.2.1 -> 3.2.2. v3.6.1: fix-up release — apply-updates 3.3.1 hoists the manifest_sync drift-detection sweep out of Phase 4's per-collection-update loop into a new unconditional Phase 4.5. Closes bug 20260511-8d20ea22 (3.6.0's Phase 4 step 3.5 was structurally unreachable when an apply-updates batch contained no collection-update operations — including the very batch that shipped 3.6.0 itself; the manifest_sync backfill promised in 3.6.0's CHANGELOG could not actually run on the upgrade that introduced the feature). The file-content sync mechanics are extracted into a standalone subroutine called from both Phase 4 step 3.5 (per-collection-update) and Phase 4.5 (unconditional sweep). v3.6.0: bug-cleanup release — apply-updates Phase 4 file-content sync (rewrites local .md/setup/manifest files during collection-update; closes bugs 20260502-8d20ea22-5 and 20260507-8d20ea22, with granular per-collection manifest_sync tracking in member-index.json); apply-updates Phase 1 step 5 extended to also strip the misplaced `exec` block from org-config.json (alongside the existing mcp_server strip); publish-updates 3.3.0 — Step 0 source-tree skip-list applied symmetrically across upload AND delete decisions plus new ephemeral-file patterns; workspace-marker-vs-collection-template Phase 2 — legacy collection-template file retired and apply-updates fallback removed; create-org template + org-config-schema.json no longer emit the duplicate exec block; gdrive credentials inline-vs-split rationale documented in schema and authoring guide. v3.5.0: publish-updates 3.2.0 with `--check-upstream` and smart prerequisite detection. v3.4.0: native Go permission-helper binary v0.2.0 + binary-distribution registry. v3.3.x: permission-change-helper skill + invite-member rewrite. v3.2.0: org-setup management dashboard. v3.1.x: extended adapter contract v2.0. v3.0.x: natural language triggers.
author: agent-index
